text.title: django-models Procedure
text.summary: Five-step procedure to bring Django models to the design rules.

list.item: Action: make every domain model inherit `core.models.BaseModel` (see `django-base-model`). Migrate by setting `Meta.abstract = True` on legacy custom bases.
Action: add `on_delete=` to every FK. Default `PROTECT`; `CASCADE` only for semantically-owned children.
Input: status / kind / role CharFields with values managed by docstring.
Action: define `TextChoices` in `apps/<app>/constants.py`; point the field at `choices=Status.choices, default=Status.PENDING`.
Output: enums typed; .choices form ready for forms.
Input: slow-query log or known composite filter patterns.
Action: add `Meta.indexes = [models.Index(fields=[...])]` covering each pattern. Run a migration.
Output: full-scan queries gone; index usage visible in `EXPLAIN`.
Input: model methods that orchestrate other models / external systems.
Action: move into `apps/<app>/services/<topic>.py`. Keep only `__str__`, property fields, and pure-self queries on the model.
Output: models are data + simple representations; orchestration lives in services.
